Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for the Pham file using search_emails (query: "Pham" or "Destiny Pham" or "closing failure" or similar). The agent discovers several critical email threads: (1) a lender conditions email from conditions@homesteadwholesale.com to sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com (CC carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com) issuing a prior-to-close condition on LN-2026-00616 requiring an updated homeowners insurance binder naming Homestead Wholesale Lending as mortgagee, due no later than 3/17; (2) an email from Allison Mercer (allison.mercer@mercerrealtygroup.com) to carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com (CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com) with subject "Destiny Pham closing failure" stating that Keystone's processor failed to clear a lender item and let the lock expire, demanding a written explanation and threatening counsel; (3) Grace Yamamoto's reply to Allison Mercer acknowledging the missed internal follow-up, file suspension, and expired lock, and stating she is taking over escalation; (4) Grace's email to Sofia, Elena, and Carlos scheduling a pipeline review to cover the 3/17 condition timeline, file suspension, lock expiration, and missed closing.
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Pham" or "LN-2026-00616" or "Destiny" or similar). The agent discovers the full internal timeline in #loan-processing (C002) and #closings (C003): (1) Sofia Reyes flagging the fresh PTC condition on LN-2026-00616 including the HOI binder due 3/17 (posted ~3/9); (2) Sofia admitting she is still underwater and hasn't touched the Destiny Pham insurance binder item, asking if the lender gives a grace period (~3/14); (3) Camille Foster reporting that LN-2026-00616 flipped to suspended on her lock list and the lock expired (~3/19); (4) Carlos Rivera saying Destiny emailed him asking if they are actually closing and he has nothing to give her (~3/19); (5) Sofia admitting she just opened the file and realized the 3/17 condition never went back, file is suspended, lock died, and closing already passed -- "I missed this" (~3/22); (6) Carlos reporting Allison Mercer came in hot threatening counsel/broker escalation (~3/22); (7) Grace telling Carlos to keep borrower-facing comms tight and not promise a close date or lock fix (~3/22); (8) Grace reassigning the file to Elena: "Elena, I'm moving LN-2026-00616 to you effective now. Suspended file, missed closing, expired lock. Please take point with lender cleanup and next steps. Carlos stays borrower-facing." (~3/22); (9) Grace scheduling a Sofia pipeline review meeting.
- Search the mortgage LOS for the Pham loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Pham" or "Destiny Pham" or similar). The agent discovers loan LN-2026-00616: conventional purchase, loan amount $290,000, purchase price $377,323, property at 7118 Nations Ford Rd, Charlotte, NC 28217, rate 6.926%, rate_lock_date 2026-03-01, rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-31, status "processing", title company Tryon Title & Escrow LLC. The agent also discovers that the LOS shows different staff assignments than what the email/Slack narrative indicates -- the assigned LO and processor in the LOS may not match the people actually working the file (Carlos Rivera and Sofia Reyes) as shown in the email and Slack evidence. The agent must reconcile this by relying on the email/Slack trail as the operational truth. The agent may also encounter other Destiny Pham loans (e.g., LN-2026-00610, LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00602, LN-2026-00605, LN-2026-00624, LN-2026-00630) and must identify LN-2026-00616 as the correct file based on the "purchase where closing fell through and lender suspended" context.
- Check the rate lock status for the Pham loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: discovered loan ID for LN-2026-00616). The agent discovers the rate lock details: rate 6.926%, lock date 2026-03-01, lock expiration 2026-03-31. Cross-referencing with Slack, the agent learns the lock effectively expired without the file being cleared -- Camille and Sofia both confirm the lock died while the file sat in suspension.
- Check conditions on the Pham loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: discovered loan ID for LN-2026-00616). The agent discovers there are no conditions recorded in the LOS for this loan. This is a critical finding -- the missed HOI binder condition that caused the entire cascade is only documented in the lender's email (from conditions@homesteadwholesale.com) and referenced in Slack, but was never entered into the LOS conditions tracker.
- Search CRM for Destiny Pham using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Destiny Pham" or similar) and then crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: [discovered CRM contact ID]). The agent discovers CRM engagement records including: (1) Carlos tried calling Destiny on closing day (3/19) but she did not answer -- he left a voicemail and privately told Grace in Slack he has no idea what to tell her because the file is still suspended; (2) Allison Mercer called Carlos before sending the written threat email (3/22) -- she was direct about the financial harm to her client, said her broker is already aware, and warned that two other Keystone referral relationships are now at risk. The agent also finds multiple Destiny Pham CRM contact records which may require disambiguation.
- Search Stripe for charges or fees on Destiny Pham using stripe_search_customers (query: "Destiny Pham" or "Pham" or similar) and then stripe_list_charges (customer_id: discovered customer ID). The agent discovers Stripe customer records for Destiny Pham linked to other loan numbers (LN-2026-00522 and LN-2026-00610). Only LN-2026-00522 has associated charges (credit report fee $60, application deposit $800, appraisal fee $475, rate lock extension $350); the LN-2026-00610 customer record exists but has zero charges. The agent finds no Stripe customer record or charges specifically tied to LN-2026-00616. This means either charges for this loan were not yet collected through Stripe, or they are tracked under a different system. The agent should report this finding.
- Look up Allison Mercer's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Allison Mercer" or "Mercer" or similar) to confirm her email address for the professional update. The agent discovers Allison Mercer with email allison.mercer@mercerrealtygroup.com, described as "Realtor with Mercer Realty Group handling Destiny Pham purchase."
- Add a comprehensive note to the loan (WRITE ACTION) using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: discovered loan ID for LN-2026-00616, action: "note" or similar, detail: comprehensive timeline and status summary). The note should document: the full timeline (lender PTC condition issued ~3/9 requiring updated HOI binder by 3/17, Sofia flagged but was overloaded, condition missed, file suspended ~3/19, rate lock expired, closing missed, Allison Mercer threatened legal action 3/22, Grace reassigned file to Elena), current status (file suspended, lock expired, closing date missed, borrower's agent threatening escalation), what went wrong (Sofia's workload prevented timely clearing of the insurance binder condition, no escalation occurred before the deadline passed), and recovery path (reinstate file by satisfying the HOI binder condition, address lock extension/re-lock with lender, coordinate new closing date).
- Send an email to Grace Yamamoto (WRITE ACTION) using send_email (sender: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com]). The email should summarize Elena's investigation findings: the root cause (Sofia missed the HOI binder condition due to workload overload, no escalation before the 3/17 lender deadline), the current file status (suspended, lock expired, closing missed), Allison Mercer's outreach and threat of legal action, the absence of conditions or activity tracking in the LOS for this loan, any Stripe charge findings, and Elena's recommended recovery plan (reinstate with lender by satisfying the original HOI binder condition, price a lock extension or re-lock, coordinate a new closing date with all parties once reinstated).
- Send a professional update email to Allison Mercer (WRITE ACTION) using send_email (sender: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [allison.mercer@mercerrealtygroup.com]). The email should introduce Elena as the new point of contact for the Destiny Pham file, acknowledge the missed closing and the disruption to her client, confirm that Elena has reviewed the full file and is working with the lender on reinstatement and lock recovery, and outline the next steps (satisfying the outstanding lender condition, securing updated pricing, establishing a new closing timeline). The tone should be professional, accountable, and action-oriented without making premature commitments on specific dates or costs.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

- Search the mortgage LOS for the Pham loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Pham" or "Destiny Pham" or similar). - Send a professional update email to Allison Mercer (WRITE ACTION) using send_email (sender: elena.marchetti@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [allison.mercer@mercerrealtygroup.com]). The email should introduce Elena as the new point of contact for the Destiny Pham file, acknowledge the missed closing and the disruption to her client, confirm that Elena has reviewed the full file and is working with the lender on reinstatement and lock recovery, and outline the next steps (satisfying the outstanding lender condition, securing updated pricing, establishing a new closing timeline). The tone should be professional, accountable, and action-oriented without making premature commitments on specific dates or costs.

1 The model must use a Mortgage LOS loan search or lookup tool (e.g., mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_loan, or similar).
tool selection
